preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.21.10: Codex CLI MCP autoconfig — Merlin's MCP sidecar is now first-class on three hosts (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Codex CLI). Pre-fix the MCP-direct sidecar (shipped v1.20.0) only autoconfigured the two Claude clients; Codex users had to hand-edit ~/.codex/config.toml in TOML, and the doc snippet they were pointed at showed JSON — the wrong format. Enterprise customers running Codex via Intune / Jamf saw zero seamless install for that population. Fix: new app/codex-config.js with a vendored minimal TOML parser/serializer (no third-party dep — supply-chain surface stays flat) that atomically merges [mcp_servers.merlin] into the user's config.toml. Unknown sections (e.g. [tui] with literal-string values) are preserved verbatim via an OPAQUE PASSTHROUGH path so we never trash the user's other config. Refuse-to-clobber on structurally unparseable input ([[array_of_tables]]). detectInstalledClients() returns {desktop, code, codex} (defensive — codex stays false if the codex-config module fails to load, so a future syntax error there can't break Claude detection). Autoprompt dialog now offers per-host buttons + 'Add to All' when ≥2 hosts are eligible. Codex has its own decision sentinel (.mcp-codex-prompt) so a 'never' on Claude doesn't suppress the Codex offer on a major bump if Codex was newly installed. Two MDM-friendly env vars added for IT-deployed Merlin installs: MERLIN_SHIM_PATH_OVERRIDE pins the shim path (useful when mirroring to an AV-excluded location), MERLIN_NODE_OVERRIDE pins the Node binary (useful for corp-signed runtimes). Both must be absolute paths or they're ignored — relative paths in MCP server entries break Codex/Claude Code on directory changes. The status IPC returns overridesActive: true so the renderer can show a 'managed by IT' badge. New IPC handler mcp-autoconfig-add-codex; mcp-autoconfig-status now returns codex: { configured, configPath, installed } alongside the existing desktop/code shape (backward-compat aliases registered/configPath still present for v1.20.0 callers). Tests: 43 new in app/codex-config.test.js (TOML round-trip, opaque preservation, refuse-to-clobber, decision sentinel, end-to-end multi-server registration, mode 0o600 atomic writes); 2 added to app/claude-desktop-config.test.js covering codex shape + load-failure resilience; all 53 + 43 + 30 + 32 (claude-desktop-config + codex-config + merlin-mcp-shim + mcp-ipc-endpoint) green. CI sweep at app/*.test.js picks up codex-config.test.js automatically — no workflow YAML edit required. Doc updates: MCP-SIDECAR-SETUP.md promoted Codex from 'other clients (manual)' to 'three clients, three config models' with TOML vs JSON shape table; manual snippet now shows BOTH JSON (Cline/Cursor) and TOML (manual Codex if not using autoconfig). No security boundary changes — vault stays inside Electron, the IPC endpoint still allowlists ping/tools/list/tools/call only.
whatsNew: Codex CLI users can now connect Merlin in one click. Pre-fix you had to hand-edit ~/.codex/config.toml in TOML format and our doc snippet showed JSON — the wrong shape. Now the autoprompt detects Codex alongside Claude Desktop and Claude Code, and writes a properly-formatted [mcp_servers.merlin] block atomically. If you keep other servers in config.toml (or have a [tui] theme block), they're preserved byte-for-byte.

Multi-host autoprompt: if you have Claude Desktop + Codex (or all three) installed, the dialog shows one button per host plus an 'Add to All' shortcut. Codex has its own per-major-version sentinel, so choosing 'never' for Claude doesn't lock Codex out of the next prompt.

Enterprise / MDM deployment: two new env vars (MERLIN_SHIM_PATH_OVERRIDE, MERLIN_NODE_OVERRIDE) let IT pin the shim and Node binary to managed paths instead of relying on installer layout. Useful for AV-exclusion mirrors, corp-signed Node runtimes, and multi-version installs. Status IPC returns overridesActive: true so the UI can show a 'managed by IT' badge.
